epics.title: Require browser-first evidence for visual UI work

tasks.title: Define which tasks count as visual UI work and what evidence they require
acceptance_criteria: The workflow rules explicitly classify styling, layout, spacing, typography, and other visual UI changes as browser-verified work.
The policy distinguishes visual tasks from non-visual implementation changes so browser checks are not required everywhere.
done_definition: Agents can determine from the docs whether browser evidence is mandatory for a given task.

tasks.title: Update agent prompts and workflow docs to make before-and-after browser checks the default for visual tasks
acceptance_criteria: Design and verification guidance describe browser checks before edits and after edits for visual work.
Repository operating rules and AI-team docs align with the same expectation.
done_definition: The browser-first rule appears consistently in the main workflow surfaces agents are expected to read.

tasks.title: Validate the updated workflow artifacts and record any remaining limits
acceptance_criteria: Updated workflow artifacts validate against the current schemas when applicable.
Remaining limits, such as places where enforcement is policy-only rather than runtime-enforced, are recorded explicitly.
done_definition: The run can complete with the workflow updates documented and validated.

assumptions: The immediate need is policy and prompt alignment rather than adding a new hard runtime enforcement step inside the supervisor.
risks: If browser-first verification remains policy-only, agents can still fail to comply without reviewer discipline.
If the visual-task definition is too loose, unnecessary browser work may be triggered for non-visual changes.
